description: Region of British Columbia where the survey was performed. Calvert Island or Quadra Island
Date on which the survey was conducted
Rocky intertidal site where survey was conducted
The individual(s) who conducted the survey
The time (24hr clock) when the survey was begun
The time (24hr clock) when the survey was completed
The tide height at the time the survey was started
The effective tide height at the time the survey was started (due to swell and wind)
Life stage category (adult/juvenile) for observed Pisaster ochraceus
Taxonomic name for a given taxon code
Common vernacular description of what a given code represents (i.e. acorn barnacle, dead acorn barnacle)
Category 0-4 (healthy-dissolved), reflecting healthiness or stage of wasting disease
Size category of radius (sea stars), total length (abalone & Cryptochiton stelleri), or test width (sea urchins)
The number of individuals of a given size category in the swath
The individual(s) who entered the data into the spreadsheet
The individual(s) who quality controlled the data
Records anything of note about a given observation
Status of the data in terms of data package progress
Area searched in two dimensions, calculated from ROV imagery
Area searched in three dimensions (i.e. including all cracks and crevices), calculated from ROV imagery
Month of the year, extracted from the date column
Year, extracted from the date column
Year and month, extracted from the date column
Swab sample ID
Swab sample ID, updated to match metadata entry in some cases so VLOOKUP would work correctly
Specifies either swab or seawater sample
Specifies either sample or negative control
Specifies sample, extraction negative or PCR negative
Combines SampleType and SampleOrNeg columns, e.g. SwabSample, SeawaterExtNeg
The type of swab used to collect the sample, either cotton or flocked
Date on which DNA was extracted from the sample
Who performed the DNA extraction
The name of the kit used for the DNA extraction
The amount of DNA detected in a particular sample
The final elution volume used in the DNA extraction
Anything noteworthy related to the sample during DNA extraction or sequencing

The associated DNA well for a particular sample
The associated sequencing run for a particular sample
Either intertidal or subtidal, indicating the type of survey which the swab was associated with
The substrate on which the host was living when the sample was collected
The host from which the swab sample was collected; usually a sea star species, but seawater also included here
The distance from the center of the sea star to the tip of it's longest arm
The associated project for a particular swab sample
The region from which the swab was collected, either Calvert or Quadra
The location from which the swab was collected, either Quadra, Outer Islands or Fjord
The standardized site ID for a particular field site using a ROCKY prefix, e.g. ROCKY12
The actual site name e.g. annex
Date on which the swab was collected
Month of the year, extracted from the date column
Year, extracted from the date column
Year and month, extracted from the date column
Day, extracted from the date column
Day of the year expressed as a number between 1 and 365
Category 0-4 (healthy-dissolved), reflecting healthiness or stage of wasting disease
One of healthy (wasting score = 0), mild (wasting score 1 or 2) or severe (wasting score 3 or 4)
The type of tissue swabbed, either H for healthy or L for lesion
A unique ID assigned to each individual sea star - some individuals might have just 1 associated swab (H), while others have two (H + L)
The seawater sterivex ID associated with a particular swab, prefix of CMIC for Calvert samples and QMIC for Quadra samples
The number of PCR cycles used in the amplication step
Life stage category (adult/juvenile) based on radius measurement, where 30mm and less assumed to be juvenile and above 30mm assumed to be adult
Yes or No, based on whether a sample made it to the DNA sequencing step
The number of sequence reads corresponding to that sample
Season category based on months, where 1 and 2 = Winter, 5 = Spring, 6, 7 and 8 = Summer and 9, 11 and 12 = Fall
Whether or not the individual star is healthy (category 0) or diseased (categories 1-4)
The combination of individual sea star disease status and type of tissue swab - one of H_healthy, H_diseased or L_diseased
The unique ASV ID which matches those in the ASV table (asvtable_NCM_smiconly-runs1to8)
The domain to which the ASV was assigned
The phylum to which the ASV was assigned
The class to which the ASV was assigned
The order to which the ASV was assigned
The family to which the ASV was assigned
The genus to which the ASV was assigned
The species to which the ASV was assigned
The number of reads of each ASV ID (rows) for each sample (columns). Sample IDs match those in the metadata file
